findings.title: No test for rule names with special characters in netsh output
findings.description: The implementation parses rule names from netsh output and embeds them as f"name={rule_name}" in subprocess list args. Since shell=True is not used, shell injection is not possible. However, there is no test covering rule names that contain characters like newlines, null bytes, or colons — which could affect the line-based parser in _list_firewall_rules_by_prefix (specifically the split(':', 1) call).
findings.impact: If a rule name contained a colon, the parser would split incorrectly. In practice, netsh rule names cannot contain colons, so this is theoretical.
findings.recommendation: Add an edge-case to NETSH_OUTPUT: a rule name with a colon (e.g., 'geo-fix-webrtc:special') to ensure split(':', 1) handles it correctly, or add a comment explaining why colons are impossible in Windows firewall rule names.

findings.title: No test verifies audit logging of firewall rule deletions
findings.description: Firewall rule deletion is a security-relevant operation. The implementation logs at logger.info level for successful deletions and logger.warning for failures. The tests do not assert that logging occurs, so a future refactor could silently remove the audit trail.
findings.impact: Loss of audit trail for firewall modifications if logging is removed in a future refactor.
findings.recommendation: Add a patch for the logger and assert logger.info is called with the count of deleted rules after remove_firewall_rules() completes.

findings.title: Fallback path silently discards all exceptions — not covered by tests
findings.description: In remove_firewall_rules(), the fallback loop (when prefix query returns empty) uses bare 'except Exception: pass', suppressing all errors silently. The test test_remove_by_prefix_fallback_on_parse_error does not verify this silent-failure behavior. If netsh delete fails in fallback mode, the user receives no notification and no log entry.
findings.impact: Firewall rules may survive cleanup silently during fallback, leaving WebRTC STUN blocking disabled without user notification.
findings.recommendation: Change the fallback exception handler from 'pass' to 'logger.warning(...)'. Add a test that verifies a warning is logged when the fallback delete call raises an exception.
